ocr.text: In the FY2023 report by China State Construction regarding Construction & Building Materials, the table detailing project
statistics reveals that Residential Projects have a BIM Adoption Rate of 85%, with an Investment Amount of £15M, yielding
Cost Savings of £3M, and are marked as Completed with a Completion Date of Q42023. Commercial Projects show a BIM
Adoption Rate of 75%, an Investment Amount of £20M, Cost Savings of £5M, are Ongoing, and will conclude in Q22024.
Infrastructure Projects have a high BIM Adoption Rate of 90%, with an Investment Amount of £12M and Cost Savings of £2M,
also Completed with a Completion Date of Q12023. Mixed-Use Projects exhibit a BIM Adoption Rate of 70%, an Investment
Amount of £18M, and Cost Savings of £4M, remain Ongoing, projected for Q32024.

CommercialProjects  75%  £20M  £5M  Ongoing  Q22024
InfrastructureProjects  90%  £12M  £2M  Completed  Q12023
Mixed-UseProjects  70%  £18M  £4M  Ongoing  Q32024
TotalProjects  80%  £65M  £14M  Varied  N/A
BIMTraining  100%  £1M  N/A  Completed  Q12023
FeedbackScore  4.5/5  N/A  N/A  N/A  N/A
FutureProjects  85%  £30M  £6M  Planned  Q42024
Total Projects have an 80% BIM Adoption Rate, with a cumulative Investment Amount of £65M and Cost Savings of £14M,
labeled as Varied with no specified Completion Date. Additionally, BIM Training achieved a 100% Adoption Rate with £1M
invested, Completed in Q12023, while the Feedback Score stands at 4.5/5, and Future Projects expect an 85% Adoption Rate
with £30M allocated and £6M in Cost Savings, Planned for Q42024.
InfrastructureSpending  AllocationBreakdown
Category  Amount(GBP)  YoYChange(%)
RoadConstruction  45M  5.2
BridgeDevelopment  30M  4.8
RailProjects  25M  6.1
Utilities  15M  3.5
TotalInvestment  115M  MarketShare(%)
In the Infrastructure Spending table, the Allocation Breakdown shows Road Construction at £45M with a YoY Change of 5.2%,
Bridge Development at £30M with a YoY Change of 4.8%, Rail Projects at £25M with a YoY Change of 6.1%, Utilities at
£15M with a YoY Change of 3.5%, culminating in a Total Investment of £115M, reflecting Market Share(%).
